non_goals: do not let audio content owners inherit device-control, format, eject, partition, firmware, or driver permissions
do not perform network research inside the music owner; consume only reviewed structured corrections
do not delete, overwrite, transcode, or rewrite media content during library organization
do not bypass exact plan confirmation, source hashes, fresh hardware binding, journals, or post-state validation

integration_policy.admission_rule: before activation, the workflow must consume a membership plan that identifies the member, owner, lifecycle, authoritative state, required surfaces, dependency path, and reload/restart boundary
integration_policy.structured_input_rule: explicit structured fields are authoritative; natural language only fills absent non-safety fields
integration_policy.batch_identity_rule: each independent item keeps a stable item_id, required flag, acceptance predicate, status, error, and retry scope through every layer
integration_policy.success_rule: transport or owner ok is not completion unless the caller's acceptance predicate is satisfied
integration_policy.consumption_rule: result-producing work is end-to-end terminal only after the caller reads or explicitly waives the owned result and records consumption
integration_policy.dependency_rule: external packages and binaries are acquired through the owning resource/package path; wrapper packages and large runtime assets remain separately authorized and verified
integration_policy.activation_rule: registration, generated config, route catalogs, runtime launch guards, maintenance baselines, and user-visible discovery must converge before activation is complete
integration_policy.optional_member_rule: optional or session-bound members remain nonblocking unless explicitly promoted to required startup capability
integration_policy.reload_rule: the contract must state whether a live reload, owner restart, Codex restart, or machine restart is required and must not claim immediate activation otherwise
integration_policy.concurrency_rule: regressions isolate test-owned state and identify test leakage by ownership; they must not require legitimate production state to remain globally static
integration_policy.change_propagation_rule: architecture changes update the contract, impact mapping, maintenance surface, validators, and closeout evidence in the same change
integration_policy.reconciliation_rule: after changed files are known, impact mapping reconciles desired contract state with actual registration, routing, runtime, maintenance, documentation, and retirement surfaces before completion is claimed
integration_policy.closeout_enforcement_rule: workflow finalization derives membership obligations from changed files and blocks a successful closeout until the membership owner validates and its receipt is present

lifecycle_policy.completion_rule: decommissioned requires every retirement surface to be satisfied or explicitly marked not_applicable with owner evidence
lifecycle_policy.history_rule: historical references may remain only in backups, checkpoints, migration sources, and archives; they must not activate, generate, route, recommend, or validate the member as current
lifecycle_policy.ownership_rule: system membership judges convergence; each surface owner applies and validates its own changes

retirement_surfaces.purpose: stable system/member/kind identity, lifecycle state, owner, reason, and effective time are explicit
replacement or explicit no-replacement decision is validated before active ownership is released
retirement_surfaces.evidence: targeted owner validation plus retirement receipt

retirement_surfaces.purpose: active executable entrypoints, launcher aliases, service wrappers, and discoverable implementation modules are removed or moved into an excluded historical archive
retirement_surfaces.evidence: targeted active-tree scan plus owner validator

retirement_surfaces.purpose: live registration, service declarations, plugin manifests, and startup requirements no longer activate the member
defaults, bootstrap, fallback, repair, and code generation cannot recreate the retired member
workflow, capability, facade, recommendation, and fallback routes no longer select the retired member
services, scheduled tasks, ports, leases, and orphan processes are stopped or explicitly classified as historical evidence
retirement_surfaces.evidence: owner runtime doctor or process inventory

retirement_surfaces.purpose: current baselines, doctors, repairs, snapshots, metrics, and health checks no longer require or recommend the retired member
current documentation, skills, prompts, and new-session guidance do not present the retired member as active
authoritative data, migration inputs, backups, and historical evidence have an explicit keep/migrate/archive/delete classification
retirement_surfaces.evidence: owner migration ledger or retention receipt

retirement_surfaces.purpose: upstream dependencies and downstream consumers are migrated, released, or explicitly blocked
a tombstone or equivalent negative contract prevents registration, generation, routing, or recommendation from returning
retirement_surfaces.evidence: owner regression test plus system membership validate

retirement_surfaces.purpose: completion records the affected surfaces, evidence, retained history, replacement, and rollback boundary

integration_policy.dependency_rule: external packages and binaries are acquired through the owning resource/package path; wrapper packages and large runtime assets remain separately authorized and verified
integration_policy.activation_rule: registration, generated config, route catalogs, runtime launch guards, maintenance baselines, and user-visible discovery must converge before activation is complete
integration_policy.optional_member_rule: optional or session-bound members remain nonblocking unless explicitly promoted to required startup capability
integration_policy.reload_rule: the contract must state whether a live reload, owner restart, Codex restart, or machine restart is required and must not claim immediate activation otherwise
integration_policy.concurrency_rule: regressions isolate test-owned state and identify test leakage by ownership; they must not require legitimate production state to remain globally static
integration_policy.change_propagation_rule: architecture changes update the contract, impact mapping, maintenance surface, validators, and closeout evidence in the same change
integration_policy.reconciliation_rule: after changed files are known, impact mapping reconciles desired contract state with actual registration, routing, runtime, maintenance, documentation, and retirement surfaces before completion is claimed
integration_policy.closeout_enforcement_rule: workflow finalization derives membership obligations from changed files and blocks a successful closeout until the membership owner validates and its receipt is present

lifecycle_policy.completion_rule: decommissioned requires every retirement surface to be satisfied or explicitly marked not_applicable with owner evidence
lifecycle_policy.history_rule: historical references may remain only in backups, checkpoints, migration sources, and archives; they must not activate, generate, route, recommend, or validate the member as current
lifecycle_policy.ownership_rule: system membership judges convergence; each surface owner applies and validates its own changes

retirement_surfaces.purpose: stable system/member/kind identity, lifecycle state, owner, reason, and effective time are explicit

retirement_surfaces.evidence: owner regression test plus system membership validate

new contract owner and validation commands are discoverable from the workspace maintenance map

do not weaken functionality to improve startup time

integration_policy.admission_rule: before activation, the workflow must consume a membership plan that identifies the member, owner, lifecycle, authoritative state, required surfaces, dependency path, and reload/restart boundary
integration_policy.structured_input_rule: explicit structured fields are authoritative; natural language only fills absent non-safety fields
integration_policy.batch_identity_rule: each independent item keeps a stable item_id, required flag, acceptance predicate, status, error, and retry scope through every layer
integration_policy.success_rule: transport or owner ok is not completion unless the caller's acceptance predicate is satisfied
integration_policy.consumption_rule: result-producing work is end-to-end terminal only after the caller reads or explicitly waives the owned result and records consumption
integration_policy.dependency_rule: external packages and binaries are acquired through the owning resource/package path; wrapper packages and large runtime assets remain separately authorized and verified
integration_policy.activation_rule: registration, generated config, route catalogs, runtime launch guards, maintenance baselines, and user-visible discovery must converge before activation is complete
integration_policy.optional_member_rule: optional or session-bound members remain nonblocking unless explicitly promoted to required startup capability
integration_policy.reload_rule: the contract must state whether a live reload, owner restart, Codex restart, or machine restart is required and must not claim immediate activation otherwise
integration_policy.concurrency_rule: regressions isolate test-owned state and identify test leakage by ownership; they must not require legitimate production state to remain globally static
integration_policy.change_propagation_rule: architecture changes update the contract, impact mapping, maintenance surface, validators, and closeout evidence in the same change
integration_policy.reconciliation_rule: after changed files are known, impact mapping reconciles desired contract state with actual registration, routing, runtime, maintenance, documentation, and retirement surfaces before completion is claimed
integration_policy.closeout_enforcement_rule: workflow finalization derives membership obligations from changed files and blocks a successful closeout until the membership owner validates and its receipt is present

lifecycle_policy.completion_rule: decommissioned requires every retirement surface to be satisfied or explicitly marked not_applicable with owner evidence
lifecycle_policy.history_rule: historical references may remain only in backups, checkpoints, migration sources, and archives; they must not activate, generate, route, recommend, or validate the member as current
lifecycle_policy.ownership_rule: system membership judges convergence; each surface owner applies and validates its own changes

retirement_surfaces.purpose: stable system/member/kind identity, lifecycle state, owner, reason, and effective time are explicit

retirement_surfaces.evidence: targeted owner validation plus retirement receipt

retirement_surfaces.purpose: active executable entrypoints, launcher aliases, service wrappers, and discoverable implementation modules are removed or moved into an excluded historical archive
retirement_surfaces.evidence: targeted active-tree scan plus owner validator

retirement_surfaces.purpose: live registration, service declarations, plugin manifests, and startup requirements no longer activate the member

workflow, capability, facade, recommendation, and fallback routes no longer select the retired member
services, scheduled tasks, ports, leases, and orphan processes are stopped or explicitly classified as historical evidence
retirement_surfaces.evidence: owner runtime doctor or process inventory

retirement_surfaces.purpose: current baselines, doctors, repairs, snapshots, metrics, and health checks no longer require or recommend the retired member

authoritative data, migration inputs, backups, and historical evidence have an explicit keep/migrate/archive/delete classification
retirement_surfaces.evidence: owner migration ledger or retention receipt

retirement_surfaces.purpose: upstream dependencies and downstream consumers are migrated, released, or explicitly blocked

retirement_surfaces.evidence: owner regression test plus system membership validate

required_surfaces.purpose: stable long-lived owner identity and lifecycle are explicit
status, plan, bootstrap, validate, handoff, cleanup-plan, and work-Git release-readiness are reusable lifecycle operations
daily authority is the WSL worktree backed by the Windows bare Git repository; mirror is a derived recovery/release product
required_surfaces.evidence: owner snapshot and work-Git release receipt

required_surfaces.purpose: Desktop persists the Windows-visible WSL UNC Git root through Electron IPC and validates disk plus live acceptance
a bounded systemd timer restores a missing WSLInterop binfmt registration and validates exact managed files plus active scheduling
the former Windows workspace retains Windows-only execution dependencies without becoming an editing or reverse-sync authority
required_surfaces.evidence: platform path snapshot plus startup baseline metadata

required_surfaces.purpose: clone/bootstrap is validation-first and does not activate host runtime
required developer CLIs are version-locked, user-local, resource-acquired, PATH-projected, and validated during WSL bootstrap
the optional Codex app-server runs as the WSL user under systemd, uses an isolated CODEX_HOME, and exposes only the user-runtime Unix socket
dirty or parallel tasks use isolated worktrees, declared-path commits, fast-forward integration, and local bare-Git receipts
Windows and WSL projections are generated from semantics without sharing writable runtime state
Git, path, tool, capability, MCP route, and bootstrap acceptance are machine-readable
handoff records target, worktree, source snapshot, activation status, validation rows, and rollback boundary
release candidate proves clean WSL worktree, matching Windows bare Git head, and one-way derivation into the recovery mirror
cleanup is explicit and never removes the default WSL distribution, mirror, or host runtime
owner commands remain discoverable through the maintenance catalog
owner changes reconcile through membership impact and closeout evidence
